name: Wireless & RF Infringement
description: Infringement scoring overrides for Wireless & RF patents. Adds RF/protocol terminology and standards-based necessary implication guidance.
scoringGuidance: Wireless patents frequently cover 3GPP/IEEE 802.11 standard-essential features. If a product claims LTE/5G/Wi-Fi compliance, it necessarily implements mandatory standard features.
RF component patents (filters, amplifiers, antenna arrays) use physical-layer terminology that maps to specific hardware blocks in any wireless device.
MIMO and beamforming patents apply broadly — any modern wireless product with multiple antennas inherently practices some MIMO techniques.
Power management in wireless devices is standards-mandated. DRX, power control, and sleep modes are required by 3GPP specifications.
necessaryImplicationGuidance: Any LTE/5G device necessarily implements: (1) OFDMA resource allocation, (2) HARQ retransmission, (3) power control loops, (4) DRX for battery management, (5) random access procedures. Any Wi-Fi device necessarily implements: (1) CSMA/CA, (2) beacon processing, (3) association/authentication, (4) power save polling.

questionOverrides.reasoningPrompt: Wireless patents are frequently standards-essential. Identify specific 3GPP/IEEE specs referenced and whether the product claims compliance with those specs.
